title: quisqualis indica var. pierrei
intro: Quisqualis indica var. pierrei, also known as Rangoon creeper, is a climber plant belonging to the family Combretaceae. It is used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Quisqualis indica var. pierrei are typically used, and they can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures for medicinal purposes.
